Viral Fusion.name: Viral Fusion
Viral Fusion.text:
Necrozma can assimilate other
creatures, and bonding into a single Pokémon.
As an Extended Action, Necrozma may bond with a
willing or helpless Pokémon, removing them from
play until they are released as another Extended
Action. Necrozma’s Movement and Jumping
capabilities become the bonded Pokémon’s, as does
Power if the bond’s is higher. Necrozma’s Athletics,
Acrobatics, Combat, Stealth, and Perception become
the bonded Pokémon’s, though they are increased
by one rank, to a maximum of 6d6. Necrozma takes
the bond’s Primary Type (or Secondary Type if its
Primary Type is Psychic) as its Secondary Type.
While bonded, the GM will designate one move as
the bonded species’ Level-Up Movelist as its
‘signature move’. Necrozma may add this move to its
learned movelist as long as it is bonded, following
normal movelist restrictions, as long as it is of a level
to learn it normally.
For Solgaleo, this move is always Sunsteel Strike; for
Lunala, this move is always Moongeist Beam. These
Pokémon have specific forms used while bonded.

Zygarde Cells.name: Zygarde Cells
Zygarde Cells.text:
Zygarde is composed of many cells,
its strength determined by how many are gathered.
Using a Zygarde Cube, as an Extended Action, 10
Cells may be formed into a 10% Zygarde, and 50
Cells may be formed into a 50% Zygarde. These may
be disassembled into the number of Cells that they
were created from as an Extended Action. 100 Cells
may form either a 10% or 50% Zygarde with the
Power Construct Ability instead of Aura Break. The
resulting Zygarde may not be disassembled, but may
be changed between 10% and 50% Formes as an
Extended Action using the Cube. The GM determines
the Nature and Level of any Zygarde formed. As an
Extended Action, a Cube may Tutor Zygarde
available Cube moves for 1 TP each.

Anchored.name: Anchored
Anchored.effect: Dhelmise has an Anchor token on the field, which occupies one square as if it is Medium sized but is otherwise ignored for cover and targeting. Dhelmise cannot move or be moved more than 3 meters away from its Anchor, willingly or otherwise. The Anchor token itself cannot be pushed, pulled, or moved against Dhelmise's will in any way. Dhelmise may shift their Anchor Token as a Swift Action, moving it to any open space within 3 meters of itself.
Anchored.bonus: When the user Shifts the Anchor, it may immediately afterwards originate a damaging attack from its Anchor if it has the appropriate action available, giving that attack the following properties: the attack's range changes to "Melee, 1 Target", the attack deals +2d6 damage, and the attack's Class changes to Physical if it wasn't already.

Battery.name: Battery
Battery.effect: The target's next Special Attack deals +2d6+4 damage. If that attack is Electric-Typed, it deals +3d6+6 more damage instead.

Beast Boost.name: Beast Boost
Beast Boost.trigger: The user’s Damaging Attack causes an opponent to Faint
Beast Boost.effect: The user receives +1 Combat Stage in their highest non-HP stat.

Berserk.name: Berserk
Berserk.trigger: The user’s drops to half HP or below for the first time in an encounter, or becomes Enraged
Berserk.effect: The user receives +1 Special Attack Combat Stage.

Chemical Romance.name: Chemical Romance
Chemical Romance.effect: Connection - Sweet Scent. Whenever the user hits a male target with Poison Gas, Smog, Sweet Scent, Toxic, or Venom Drench, that target becomes Infatuated with the user.

Comatose.name: Comatose
Comatose.effect: The user falls Asleep, and regains a Tick of Hit Points.
Comatose.bonus: The user may act normally while Asleep and ignores all other penalties from being Asleep. While Asleep, the user does not make Save Checks to wake up, and wakes up from being hit only if they want to.

Corrosion.name: Corrosion
Corrosion.effect: The user's Poison-Type attacks are resisted one step less, and deal damage to Immune targets as if they were doubly resisted. The user may Poison and Badly Poison Steel and Poison-Type Pokémon.

Dancer.name: Dancer
Dancer.trigger: Someone within 10 meters uses a Status- Class Dance Move
Dancer.effect: The user may immediately use the triggering Move as a Free Action.

Dazzling.name: Dazzling
Dazzling.effect: For the rest of the Scene, the target's Initiative is lowered by 10 and cannot use Priority Moves.
Dazzling.bonus: Interrupt Moves may not be declared in response to the user's actions.

Disguise.name: Disguise
Disguise.trigger: The user is hit by a Damaging Move.
Disguise.effect: The triggering attack instead misses and has no effect. The user then gains +1 CS in a Stat of their choice. Defensive.

Electric Surge.name: Electric Surge
Electric Surge.effect: The Field becomes Electrified, as if affected by the Move Electric Terrain, for one full round.

Emergency Exit.name: Emergency Exit
Emergency Exit.effect: When the user’s Hit Points drop below half their maximum, their trainer may immediately recall the user and send out another Pokémon as a Free Action. If the user hasn’t taken their turn yet, their replacement may act this round. Defensive.
Emergency Exit.bonus: The user is immune to being Trapped.

Fluffy.name: Fluffy
Fluffy.effect: The user resists damaging Melee attacks one step further, but resists Fire-Type attacks one step less. Defensive.

Full Metal Body.name: Full Metal Body
Full Metal Body.effect: The user’s Combat Stages may not be lowered by the effect of foes’ Features, Abilities, or Moves. Status Afflictions may still alter their Combat Stages. Defensive.

Galvanize.name: Galvanize
Galvanize.trigger: The user uses a Normal-Type damaging Move
Galvanize.effect: The Move is changed to be Electric-Type.

Glisten.name: Glisten
Glisten.effect: The user is immune to Fairy-Type attacks. Defensive.
Glisten.bonus: If the user is hit by a damaging Fairy-Type attack, they receive +1 Defense or Special Defense Combat Stages.

Grassy Surge.name: Grassy Surge
Grassy Surge.effect: The Field becomes Grassy, as if affected by the Move Grassy Terrain, for one full round.

Handyman.name: Handyman
Handyman.effect: The user may hold two Held Items at once. Whenever an Ability or Move affects the user’s Held Items, you may choose which one is affected.

Horde Break.name: Horde Break
Horde Break.trigger: The user changes from School Form to Solo Form
Horde Break.effect: The user is cured of all Status Conditions.

Innards Out.name: Innards Out
Innards Out.trigger: The user is hit by a Damaging Attack
Innards Out.effect: The user resists the triggering attack one step further. After the attack is resolved, a foe within 2 meters loses Hit Points equal to twice the amount of Hit Points lost by the user from the triggering attack, even if the triggering attack caused the user to Faint.

Liquid Voice.name: Liquid Voice
Liquid Voice.trigger: The user uses a Move with the Sonic keyword
Liquid Voice.effect: The triggering move loses the Sonic keyword, but gains the Friendly keyword and becomes Water Typed. If the Move was a Status Class Move, you may treat it as a Special Move with DB1.

Long Reach.name: Long Reach
Long Reach.effect: The user may use damaging attacks as if they had a range of "8, 1 Target" instead of their usual range.

Merciless.name: Merciless
Merciless.effect: If the user hits a poisoned target with a damaging attack, it automatically becomes a Critical Hit.

Misty Surge.name: Misty Surge
Misty Surge.effect: The Field becomes Misty, as if affected by the Move Misty Terrain, for one full round.

Mud Shield.name: Mud Shield
Mud Shield.effect: The user gains two ticks worth of Temporary Hit Points.
Mud Shield.bonus: When standing on any muddy or dirty terrain that is either Slow or Rough Terrain, the user gains +5 Damage Reduction. Defensive.

Neuroforce.name: Neuroforce
Neuroforce.effect: Whenever you deal Super-Effective Damage to a target, that target treats your Damage Roll as it were increased by +10.

Power Construct.name: Power Construct
Power Construct.special: The user still uses the HP total and HP Maximum of the Forme that it was in (10% or 50% Forme) before entering Complete Forme. Both Formes must still follow BSR.
Power Construct.effect: The user changes to Complete Forme until the end of the Scene, and gains Temporary Hit Points equal to half of the maximum hit points that Complete Forme would have. The user cannot gain Temporary Hit Points from other sources while in Complete Forme.

Power of Alchemy.name: Power of Alchemy
Power of Alchemy.effect: The Pokémon gains an Ability known by the Target for the remainder of the encounter, or until it is Fainted.

Prism Armor.name: Prism Armor
Prism Armor.effect: The user gains +5 Damage Reduction against Super Effective-Damage. Defensive.

Psychic Surge.name: Psychic Surge
Psychic Surge.effect: The Field becomes Weird, as if affected by the Move Psychic Terrain, for one full round.

Queenly Majesty.name: Queenly Majesty
Queenly Majesty.trigger: An adjacent foe uses a Priority Move or an Interrupt action
Queenly Majesty.effect: Connection - Stomp. The user may attack the triggering foe with Stomp as a Free Action Interrupt.
Queenly Majesty.bonus: The user cannot be targeted by Interrupt Moves and Interrupts may not be declared in response to the user's actions.

Radiant Beam.name: Radiant Beam
Radiant Beam.effect: The user may use damaging Grass-Type attacks as if they had a range of "Line 4" instead of their usual range.

Receiver.name: Receiver
Receiver.effect: Each effect below may be used once per scene when triggered: • When an ally faints, the user selects one of their Abilities. The user gains that Ability until the end of the encounter. • When the user faints, they may grant use of one of their Basic Abilities to an ally on the field for the rest of the encounter.

Revelation.name: Revelation
Revelation.trigger: Someone within 10 meters uses a Damaging Dance Move
Revelation.effect: The user may immediately use the triggering Move as a Standard Action interrupt.
Revelation.bonus: Whenever the user uses a Dance Move, they gain +2 to Accuracy Rolls until the end of their next turn.

RKS System.name: RKS System
RKS System.effect: If the user is hit by a damaging attack, they may calculate damage as if they were Normal-Type instead. If they were already Normal-Type, they instead resist the damage one step further.
RKS System.bonus: The user’s Type changes to match the Type of their held Memory Disc.

Schooling.name: Schooling
Schooling.effect: The user changes to Schooling Forme, and gains Temporary Hit Points equal to half of its own maximum Hit Points. The user cannot gain Temporary Hit Points from other sources while in Schooling Forme. When the user is below half Maximum HP and has no Temporary Hit Points left, they change back into Solo Forme.
Schooling.bonus: The user has two sets of base stats; Solo and Schooling. The HP of both forms must be the same.

Shadow Shield.name: Shadow Shield
Shadow Shield.effect: While at full Hit Points, the user resists all damage one step further. Defensive.

Shields Down.name: Shields Down
Shields Down.effect: The user has two sets of base stats; Meteor and Core. The HP of both forms must be the same. When the user is in Meteor Forme and are reduced to half Maximum HP or lower, they change to Core Forme. The user returns to Meteor Forme while outside of combat if above half Maximum HP.

Slush Rush.name: Slush Rush
Slush Rush.effect: While in Hailing Weather or under half maximum Hit Points, the user’s Initiative is doubled.

Soul Heart.name: Soul Heart
Soul Heart.trigger: A combatant in the encounter Faints
Soul Heart.effect: The user receives +2 Special Attack Combat Stages and gains a tick of Temporary Hit Points.

Stakeout.name: Stakeout
Stakeout.effect: The user gains a +2d6+4 bonus on damage rolls against foes that were released from a Pokéball or entered the encounter since the user's last turn.

Stamina.name: Stamina
Stamina.trigger: The user is hit by a Damaging Attack
Stamina.effect: The user receives +1 Defense Combat Stage. Defensive.

Steelworker.name: Steelworker
Steelworker.trigger: The user takes damage while adjacent to its Anchor
Steelworker.effect: The user calculates damage as if it was only Steel-Typed.
Steelworker.bonus: The user gains STAB on Steel-Type Moves that originate from its Anchor.

Surge Surfer.name: Surge Surfer
Surge Surfer.effect: On Electric Terrain or while under half maximum Hit Points, the user’s Initiative is doubled.

Tangling Hair.name: Tangling Hair
Tangling Hair.trigger: The user is hit by a Melee Attack
Tangling Hair.effect: The attacker receives -1 Speed Combat Stage and is Slowed until the end of its next turn.

Triage.name: Triage
Triage.effect: The user’s moves with the Healing keyword may be used as Priority moves.

Water Bubble.name: Water Bubble
Water Bubble.effect: The user resists Fire-Type attacks one step further, is immune to being Burned, and may always act as though in Rainy Weather. Defensive.
Water Bubble.bonus: The user may attack with Water-Type Moves as if they had a range of “Melee, 1 target”. If they do, that Move's Class is changed to Physical and it deals +1d6+2 damage.

Water Compaction.name: Water Compaction
Water Compaction.trigger: The user is hit by a Water Type Move
Water Compaction.effect: The user receives +2 Defense Combat Stages. Defensive.

Wily.name: Wily
Wily.effect: The user's X-target Status Class Moves may target an additional target within range. Adjusted Abilities:

Accelerate.name: Accelerate
Accelerate.effect: The user may use a damaging Move to which they add STAB as a Priority Move. If the Move hits, the user adds half their Speed Stat to the Damage Roll. If the Move already had Priority, the attack gains a +4 Bonus to Accuracy. Replaces: Maelstrom Pulse, Leaf Rush, & Gale Wings

Frisk.name: Frisk
Frisk.effect: The user gains a +2 Bonus to all Accuracy Rolls against adjacent targets.

Gale Wings.name: Gale Wings
Gale Wings.effect: Connection - Quick Attack. The user may use Quick Attack as a Flying-Type Move.

Unaware.name: Unaware
Unaware.effect: Once per Scene, Pollen Puff may instead be used on an ally; when used this way, the target recovers 50% of their maximum Hit Point s and the Move has the Healing keyword. Contest Type: Beauty Contest Effect: Reflective Appeal

Dark Void.name: Dark Void
Dark Void.effect: The target falls Asleep. Once per Scene, Dark Void may be used as if its range were “Burst 5, Friendly” instead.

Darkest Lariat.name: Darkest Lariat
Darkest Lariat.effect: This Move ignores the target’s positive Defense Combat Stages and all Damage Reduction.

Hyperspace Fury.name: Hyperspace Fury
Hyperspace Fury.effect: Interrupts may not be triggered against Hyperspace Fury. The user’s Defense is lowered by -1 Combat Stage.

Power Trip.name: Power Trip
Power Trip.effect: This Move gains +2 to its Damage Base for every positive Combat Stage held by the user, to a maximum of DB 20.

Sucker Punch.name: Sucker Punch
Sucker Punch.effect: If an adjacent foe targets the user with a Damaging Attack, Sucker Punch may be used as an Interrupt Move against the triggering foe.

Throat Chop.name: Throat Chop
Throat Chop.effect: The target cannot use Moves with the Sonic keyword for the next two turns.

Clanging Scales.name: Clanging Scales
Clanging Scales.effect: The user adds half their Defense Stat to Clanging Scale's Damage Roll. After the Move is resolved, the user's Defense is lowered by 1 CS.

Core Enforcer.name: Core Enforcer
Core Enforcer.effect: Each target has its Ability disabled until the end of the encounter. If a target has more than one Ability, you choose one of them to disable.

Magnetic Flux.name: Magnetic Flux
Magnetic Flux.effect: All targets with the Plus or Minus Abilities receive +1 Defense and Special Defense Combat Stages.

Parabolic Charge.name: Parabolic Charge
Parabolic Charge.effect: After the targets take damage, the user gains Hit Points equal to half of the total damage they dealt to all targets.

Plasma Fists.name: Plasma Fists
Plasma Fists.effect: The target’s next damaging Normal Type Move instead becomes Electric Type.

Thunder Wave.name: Thunder Wave
Thunder Wave.effect: Thunder Wave Paralyzes the target. Targets immune to Electric Attacks are immune to Thunder Wave’s effects.

Zing Zap.name: Zing Zap
Zing Zap.effect: Zing Zap Flinches the target on 15+.

Fleur Cannon.name: Fleur Cannon
Fleur Cannon.effect: Lower the user’s Special Attack 2 Combat Stages after damage.

Floral Healing.name: Floral Healing
Floral Healing.effect: The target regains Hit Points equal to half of its full Hit Point value. If the field is Grassy Terrain , the target gains 2/3 of its full Hit Point value instead.

Nature’s Madness.name: Nature’s Madness
Nature’s Madness.effect: The target loses 1/2 of their current Hit Points.

Flying Press.name: Flying Press
Flying Press.effect: Flying Press may deal Flying Type damage if the user wishes.
Flying Press.contest_effect: Big Show Note: If Flying Press is Move Sync’d, it only changes the Fighting Type portion of the Move. You can still only choose between that Type and Flying Type; you cannot shift Flying Press to change the Flying part to another Type.

Burn Up.name: Burn Up
Burn Up.effect: Until the end of the encounter, the user loses its Fire -Type. P ure Fire -Types instead become Normal-Type.

Fire Lash.name: Fire Lash
Fire Lash.effect: The target’s Defense is lowered by 1 Combat Stage.

Mind Blown.name: Mind Blown
Mind Blown.effect: The user’s Hit Points are reduced by 50% of their full Hit Point value. This Hit Point loss cannot be prevented or reduced in any way.

Shell Trap.name: Shell Trap
Shell Trap.effect: If the user is hit by a Melee attack, they may use Shell Trap as an Interrupt.

Beak Blast.name: Beak Blast
Beak Blast.effect: Beak Blast must be declared at the start of the round. If the user is hit by a Melee attack this round, their attacker is Burned. At the end of the round, the user may shift and attack with Beak Blast.

Moongeist Beam.name: Moongeist Beam
Moongeist Beam.effect: This Move ignores any Abilities that would cause it to fail or miss , such as Dodge or Wonder Guard; those Abilities cannot be activated in response to Moongeist Beam.

Shadow Bone.name: Shadow Bone
Shadow Bone.effect: The target’s Defense is lowered by 1 Combat Stage on a 17+.

Spectral Thief.name: Spectral Thief
Spectral Thief.effect: Before rolling damage, transfer all Combat Stages on the target to the user.

Spirit Shackle.name: Spirit Shackle
Spirit Shackle.effect: The Target is Trapped for 2 rounds.

Solar Blade.name: Solar Blade
Solar Blade.range: Melee, 1 Target, Set Up Set-Up Effect: If the weather is not Sunny, the user gains +1 Combat Stage in Attack and Special Defense, and the user’s turn ends. If the weather is Sunny, immediately proceed to the Resolution Effect instead and this Move loses the Set-Up keyword.
Solar Blade.effect: The user attacks with Solar Blade. If the weather is Rainy, Sandstorming, or Hailing, Solar Blade’s Damage Base is lowered to 6 (2d6+8 / 15).

Strength Sap.name: Strength Sap
Strength Sap.effect: The user gains Hit Points equal to the higher of the target's Attack or Special Attack; the target then loses 1 Combat Stage in that Stat.

Trop Kick.name: Trop Kick
Trop Kick.effect: The target receives a -5 penalty to damage rolls for 1 round.

High Horsepower.name: High Horsepower
High Horsepower.effect: High Horsepower may be used as a Free Action at the end of a Sprint Maneuver taken as a Standard Action, as long as the user Shifted at least 3 meters in a straight line towards the target. When used this way, High Horsepower gains Smite.

Shore Up.name: Shore Up
Shore Up.effect: The user regains Hit Points equal to half of its full Hit Point value. If it is Sand Storming , the user gains 2/3 of its full Hit Point value instead. If it is Sunny, Rainy or Hailing the user gains 1/4 of their full Hit Point value instead.

Stomping Tantrum.name: Stomping Tantrum
Stomping Tantrum.effect: If the user’s last attack failed or missed, Stomping Tantrum is DB 15 (4d10+10 / 45) instead.

Thousand Arrows.name: Thousand Arrows
Thousand Arrows.effect: When calculating Weakness and Resistance for Thousand Arrows , Flying-Typed targ ets calculate damage as if Flying was neutral to Ground . Thousand Arrows ignores the Levitate ability. The target is knocked down to ground level, and loses all Sky or Levitate Speeds for 3 turns. During this time, they may be hit by Ground -Type Moves even if normally immune.

Thousand Waves.name: Thousand Waves
Thousand Waves.effect: Legal Targets are Trapped for 2 rounds.

Aurora Veil.name: Aurora Veil
Aurora Veil.effect: Blessing - Any user affected by Aurora Veil may activate it when receiving Damage to resist the Damage one step. Aurora Veil may be activated 2 times, and then disappears.
Aurora Veil.special: Aurora Veil can only be used when the user is in Hailing Weather. The Blessings persist even after Hailing Weather has ended, however.

Ice Hammer.name: Ice Hammer
Ice Hammer.effect: The user’s Speed is lowered by 1 Combat Stage.

Laser Focus.name: Laser Focus
Laser Focus.effect: The next successful damaging attack made by the user is automatically a Critical Hit.

Multi-Attack.name: Multi-Attack
Multi-Attack.effect: Multi-Attack is the same Type as the Memory Disc, Plate, or Drive Item held.

Revelation Dance.name: Revelation Dance
Revelation Dance.effect: Revelation Dance is the same Type as the user’s primary Type (the first one listed in the Pokédex). Revelation Dance deals +5 Bonus Damage for every other Da nce Move used by the user this round, to a maximum of +15.

Spotlight.name: Spotlight
Spotlight.effect: Until the end of the user’s next turn, the target is Blinded and Vulnerable.

Swagger.name: Swagger
Swagger.effect: The target’s Attack is raised 2 Combat Stages. Swagger Confuses the target.

Tearful Look.name: Tearful Look
Tearful Look.effect: Tearful Look lowers all Legal Targets ’ Attack and Special Attack 1 Combat Stage.

Baneful Bunker.name: Baneful Bunker
Baneful Bunker.effect: If the user is hit by an attack, the user may use Baneful Bunker. The user is instead not hit by the Move. You do not take any damage nor are you affected by any of the Move’s effects. In addition, if the triggering attack was Melee ranged, the attacker is Poisoned.

Purify.name: Purify
Purify.effect: The target is cured of all Permanent and Volatile Statuses. For every status removed, the user recovers a Tick of Hit Points.

Toxic Thread.name: Toxic Thread
Toxic Thread.effect: The target is Poisoned and has their Speed lowered by 1 Combat Stage . If the target is already Poisoned, they instead lose a Tick of Hit Points and has their Speed lowered by 2 Combat Stages.

Instruct.name: Instruct
Instruct.effect: The target immediately reuses the attack it performed last, ignoring frequency, as a Free Action. They may cho ose new targets for the copied attack. Instruct may not be used if the Target’s last Move was a Set-Up Move, a Trigger Move, or Instruct, or if the Target is currently affected by Exhaust.

Hyperspace Hole.name: Hyperspace Hole
Hyperspace Hole.effect: Make and resolve of Hyperspace Hole’s attacks separately. In between each attack, the user may Shift using their Teleporter capability, ending the Shift next to a legal target. Interrupts may not be triggered against Hyperspace Hole.

Photon Geyser.name: Photon Geyser
Photon Geyser.effect: This attack uses the highest of the user’s Attack or Special Attack Stat, though it remains a Special Move. This Move ignores any Abilities that would cause it to fail or miss , such as Dodge or Wonder Guard; those Abilities cannot be activate d in response to Photon Geyser.

Psychic Fangs.name: Psychic Fangs
Psychic Fangs.effect: Light Screen, Reflect and Aurora Veil cannot be declared in response to Psychic Fangs.

Psychic Terrain.name: Psychic Terrain
Psychic Terrain.effect: The Field becomes Weird for five rounds. While the Field is Weird, non -Flying and non - Levitating Pokémon cannot declare Priority or Interrupt Moves outside their own Initiatives. Damaging Psychic-Type attacks deal an additional 10 damage

Speed Swap.name: Speed Swap
Speed Swap.effect: The user and the target trade Initiative values.

Anchor Shot.name: Anchor Shot
Anchor Shot.effect: The Target is Trapped for 2 rounds. Anchor Shot may only be used from the user's Anchor Token.

Gear Up.name: Gear Up
Gear Up.effect: All targets with the Plus or Minus Abilities receive +1 Attack and Special Attack Combat Stages

Sunsteel Strike.name: Sunsteel Strike
Sunsteel Strike.effect: This Move ignores any Abilities that would cause it to fail or miss , such as Dodge or Wonder Guard; those Abilities cannot be activated in response to Sunsteel Strike.

Liquidation.name: Liquidation
Liquidation.effect: The target’s Defense is lowered by 1 Combat Stage on a 17+.

Sparkling Aria.name: Sparkling Aria
Sparkling Aria.effect: When you hit a target with Sparkling Aria, the user may choose to deal no damage and instead cure that target of Burn, Confusion, Infatuation, or Rage.

Steam Eruption.name: Steam Eruption
Steam Eruption.effect: Steam Eruption burns all legal targets on a 15+.
